sections.markdown:
- This was planned as a `7 km` easy run at the start of the `2026-05-18 to 2026-05-24` build week. The completed run matched that intent closely at `7.178 km`, with Strava moving time `43:36`, elapsed time `44:24`, and Apple Health linked to the same workout window.
- The execution was appropriately easy. Strava averaged `139.2 bpm` with a `155 bpm` max, Apple Health averaged `138.4 bpm` with a `154.5 bpm` max, and the route stream spent no time above Z2. This was a useful aerobic primer, not hidden quality work.

- The week is now `7.178 km` complete against the planned `76 km`, with Monday's running volume landed without borrowing from Tuesday's `16 km` half-marathon-pace workout.
- The bigger short-term load item is the later Monday basketball session: Apple Health recorded `58:56`, `3.682 km`, `149.6 bpm` average HR, and a `193 bpm` max. Keep Tuesday's workout protected, but do not add any catch-up volume or leg work around it; if the court session left the calves, adductors, or ankles flat, preserve the quality by keeping the warm-up conservative and cutting optional extras first.

- For the `2026-06-21` Parramatta Half Marathon `1:45:00` target, this did the job: aerobic volume was added without turning Monday into another hard run before the half-marathon-pace session.
- For the `2026-08-30` Sydney Marathon `3:30:00` target, this is the kind of easy-volume compliance the bigger block needs. It is not a new fitness marker by itself; the value is that it starts a higher-volume week without compromising Tuesday, Thursday, or Sunday.

- Strava moving pace averaged `6:04 /km`; elapsed pace was about `6:11 /km` after `0:48` of stoppage cost.
- The first half averaged about `6:04 /km` and the second half about `6:08 /km`. The three thirds were about `6:09`, `6:01`, and `6:07 /km`, so the pacing stayed even enough for an easy run despite the rolling route.

- Strava HR averaged `139.2 bpm` with a `155 bpm` max; Apple Health averaged `138.4 bpm` with a `154.5 bpm` max.
- Using the current LTHR app bands, the route stream spent about `39:10` in Z1, `5:14` in Z2, and no time in Z3 or above. HR rose from about `136.2 bpm` in the first half to `141.2 bpm` in the second half, which is acceptable for the climbing profile and still firmly easy.

- Strava moving time was `43:36` out of `44:24` elapsed. The route stream shows about `0:38` stopped across `4` stop groups, with only one longer than `10 s`.
- The stop cost is small enough that the pacing and HR read is reliable. This was a continuous easy run, not a fragmented session.

- Strava logged `79.0 m` of elevation gain, and the measured route model found `79.9 m` ascent with `79.4 m` descent and `high` reliability.
- GAP shows the route was a little more demanding than raw pace: raw moving pace was `6:04 /km`, Strava GAP was `5:59 /km`, and measured local GAP was about `5:56 /km`. That supports reading the effort as easy-aerobic rather than judging it only from raw pace.

- Apple Health cadence averaged `166 spm` across `1028` samples, with a median of `170 spm` and a 10th-to-90th percentile range of about `156-176 spm`.
- Cadence looks stable enough for an easy run with a short stopped section. There is no clear mechanics warning in the cadence data.

sections.heading: Weather Gear And Hidden Intensity
sections.markdown:
- Conditions were mildly awkward but not limiting: light drizzle, about `18.1 C` average temperature, `19.6 C` apparent temperature, `93%` humidity, `0.1 mm` precipitation, `6.3 kph` wind, and `13.7 kph` gusts.
- Shoes were `HOKA Bondi 9`, not retired, with about `527.3 km` in Strava's gear record and `518.8 km` in the generated public shoe graph. Strava recorded several local segment achievements, including PRs on `North Road Upwards`, `Library Dash`, and `Tindarra Dash`, but those efforts sat around `139-144 bpm` average HR, so they read as route artefacts rather than hidden hard running.

sections.heading: Recovery Context
sections.markdown:
- Apple Health recovery context was available: recent resting HR averaged `48.9 bpm`, HRV averaged `62.3 ms`, VO2 max averaged `52.7 ml/kg/min`, and the sleep-duration projection averaged `4.5 h` in the sampled window.
- The run was controlled enough to support the planned week. The only caution is cumulative load: low sleep plus Monday basketball makes Tuesday freshness the deciding signal, not this run's aerobic cost.
